items.hook: William Mason Turner is an American former professional football player who was an offensive tackle in the National Football League (NFL).
items.body: He was selected by the Miami Dolphins in the third round of the 2014 NFL draft. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Most closely associated with Celtic, he was a member of their Lisbon Lions side which won the 1967 European Cup.
items.body: James Philip Craig is a Scottish former footballer, who played as a right back. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Great Comet of 1556 was a comet that first appeared in February 1556, and which was observed throughout much of Europe.
items.body: The comet appears to have been seen in some places before the end of February, but it was not generally observed until the middle of the first week in March. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Ferrari take their first Formula One grand prix victory at the British Grand Prix at Silverstone.
items.body: Ferrari S.p.A, currently competing as Scuderia Ferrari HP, is the racing division of luxury Italian auto manufacturer Ferrari and the racing team that competes in Formula One racing. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: In the 14 July Revolution in Iraq, the monarchy is overthrown by popular forces led by Abd al-Karim Qasim, who becomes the nation's new leader.
items.body: The 14 July Revolution, also known as the 1958 Iraqi military coup, was a military coup that took place on 14 July 1958 in Iraq, resulting in the toppling of King Faisal II and the overthrow of the Hashemite-led Kingdom of Iraq. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Karlovy Vary film festival unveils 2026 winners
items.body: Winners included ‘Fruit Gathering’, ‘The Guest’ and ’A Happy Family’.

items.hook: It was more than 200 metres long, and consisted of four arcades whose walls were covered with "stucco decoration studded with mirrors, and whitewashed with a special solution containing bits of mica to make them sparkle".
items.body: The Char Chatta Bazaar of Kabul was a covered marketplace in Kabul, Afghanistan, built in the 17th century by Ali Mardan Khan, the Mughal governor of Kabul during the reign of Shah Jahan. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Ernst Johan Kuipers is a Dutch gastroenterologist, professor and politician who served as Minister of Health, Welfare and Sport in the fourth Rutte cabinet from 2022 until 2024.
items.body: He is a member of the Democrats 66 (D66) party. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Created by writers Stan Lee and Robert Bernstein, and designed by artist Don Heck, the character first appeared in Tales of Suspense #45.
items.body: Virginia "Pepper" Potts is a character appearing in American comic books published by Marvel Comics. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Disney’s ‘Moana’ tops UK-Ireland box office with £2.8m as ‘Toy Story 5’ passes £40m
items.body: Meanwhile, Studiocanal’s ’Evil Dead Burn’ opened in fourth place.

items.hook: Battle of Vítkov Hill, decisive victory of Czech Hussite forces commanded by Jan Žižka against Crusade army led by Sigismund, Holy Roman Emperor.
items.body: The Battle of Vítkov Hill was a part of the Hussite Wars. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: It hosts fundamental research as well a research for various application domains.
items.body: The Max Planck Institute for Informatics is a research institute in computer science with a focus on algorithms and their applications in a broad sense. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Louis VIII becomes King of France upon the death of his father, Philip II.
items.body: Louis VIII, nicknamed The Lion, was King of France from 1223 to 1226. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: It was published by the Propaganda Fide in Rome in 1651, upon Rhodes's visit to Europe, along with his catechism Phép giảng tám ngày.
items.body: The Dictionarium Annamiticum Lusitanum et Latinum is a trilingual Vietnamese-Portuguese-Latin dictionary written by the French Jesuit lexicographer Alexandre de Rhodes after 12 years in Vietnam. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: BBC pay 2025-2026: The full list of star salaries
items.body: Scott Mills and Greg James top the list of the best-paid BBC presenters in the last financial year.

items.hook: Arden2ByteCode is a libreware compiler for the Arden Syntax markup language.
items.body: The compiler translates HL7 International Arden Syntax for Medical Logic Modules (MLMs) to Java Bytecode. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: El Fenn is a boutique hotel in Marrakech, Morocco, opposite the Koutoubia Mosque.
items.body: El Fenn has often been listed as one of the best hotels in the world by Condé Nast Traveler being included on their Gold List in 2017, 2021, 2022, and 2024. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Finnish War: the Battle of Lapua is fought.
items.body: The Finnish War was fought between the Kingdom of Sweden and the Russian Empire from 21 February 1808 to 17 September 1809 as part of the Napoleonic Wars. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Scott Mills was highest paid BBC star before sacking
items.body: The former Radio 2 breakfast host earned about £745,000 from the BBC in the last financial year.

items.hook: Review: Foam of Worlds - FIDMarseille 2026
items.body: Anna Marziano sets an intimate mother-daughter relationship - and a lawsuit asserting the right to environmental protection - against the backdrop of the Venice Lagoon which is under threat

items.hook: Rus Flight 9633 crashes during takeoff from Chkalovsky Airport, killing all 10 people on board.
items.body: Rus Flight 9633 refers to the crash involving an IL-76TD aircraft operated by Rus Airlines from Chkalovsky Airport to Taiyuan Wusu Airport with intermediate stops at Alykel Airport and Bratsk Airport. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Bectu head Philippa Childs to leave UK union
items.body: She will stand down in January 2027.
